text: Antibiotic resistance is becoming a significant obstacle to modern wound care. Herein, we designed an antibiotic-free and sustainable antimicrobial strategy to advance wound healing using hybrid nanozymes. We developed an efficient method for the in-situ growth of ultra-small gold nanoparticles (Au NPs) onto Metal-organic Framework (MOF) immobilized $Fe_3O_4$ nanoparticles and coined them as Fe-MOF-Au (FMA) NPs. The hybrids exhibit both POD-mimicking activity and produce large amounts of .OH at a low concentration of $H_2O_2$ (0.97 mM). Importantly, the FMA NPs showed good biocompatibility as well as excellent antibacterial effects against both Gram-negative bacteria (E. coli) and Gram-positive bacteria (S. aureus) while enhancing wound healing and safety in vivo. In summary, this highly defined hybrid nanozymes technology holds great promise for the development of safe, efficient antibacterial drugs free from the risks of antibiotic-resistant bacteria.
